fields.definition_from_readme: One of eight power technology groups (coal, oil/gas, utility-scale solar, wind, hydropower, nuclear, bioenergy, geothermal)
The Country/area column presents energy data within various economic contexts, and the geographical unit does not strictly follow political boundaries
One of 17 GEM-defined subregions of the world
One of five GEM-defined world regions
Commonly used name
fields.notes_from_readme: Preference is for a government provided name. Without that information, the name used by the owner or operating company is listed

fields.definition_from_readme: Name or number of phase in plant/project
fields.notes_from_readme: Roman numerals are changed to numbers. If the owner uses non-sequential numbers or other characters, those are used

fields.definition_from_readme: Alternative names, including the name in local script
Total nameplate capacity, or best available data, of the project, in megawatts
fields.notes_from_readme: Units: MW.

fields.definition_from_readme: One of 8 status categories, see rows 112-121 below for description

value_definitions.name: announced
value_definitions.definition: Projects that have been publicly reported in corporate or governmental planning documents or media coverage but not yet actively moving forward by applying for permits or seeking land, material, or financing.

value_definitions.name: pre-construction
value_definitions.definition: Projects that are actively moving forward in seeking governmental approvals, land rights, or financing (the sum of 'pre-permit' and 'permitted' statuses in the Global Coal Plant Tracker)

value_definitions.name: construction
value_definitions.definition: Projects where site preparation and/or equipment installation are underway.

value_definitions.name: shelved
value_definitions.definition: Suspension of the project has been announced, or no progress has been observed for at least two years.

value_definitions.name: shelved - inferred 2 y
value_definitions.definition: Inferred status based on lack of observed progress for 2 years

value_definitions.name: operating
value_definitions.definition: The project has been formally commissioned; commercial operation has begun.

value_definitions.name: cancelled
value_definitions.definition: A cancellation announcement for the project has been made, or no progress has been observed for at least four years.

value_definitions.name: cancelled - inferred 4 y
value_definitions.definition: Inferred status based on lack of observed progress for 4 years

value_definitions.name: mothballed
value_definitions.definition: The project is disused, but not dismantled.

value_definitions.name: retired
value_definitions.definition: The project has been decommissioned or dismantled; this term is also used if the plant has been destroyed by war.

fields.definition_from_readme: Year the project was or will be commissioned
fields.notes_from_readme: Units: YYYY.

fields.definition_from_readme: Year the project was or will be taken offline
fields.notes_from_readme: Units: YYYY.

fields.definition_from_readme: See rows 124-182 below for description

top_values: fossil gas: natural gas, fossil liquids: fuel oil
fossil gas: natural gas, fossil liquids: diesel
fossil gas: natural gas, fossil liquids: heavy fuel oil
bioenergy: agricultural waste (solids), bioenergy: wood & other biomass (solids)
fields.definition_from_readme: See rows 185-239 below for description of fuels.
Fuel classification options include: Oil only, LNG only, Gas only, Multi fuel. Note "Gas only" does not include LNG.
Equpment manufacturer and model.
Value of 'yes' if a project phase is dedicated for green hydrogen production, 'no' if not, 'not found' or blank no data found. Only used for wind, solar, and geothermal trackers

Hydrogen capable: turbine - The turbine at the gas plant is capable of burning some % of hydrogen. 
Hydrogen capable: progress - The gas plant has made some sort of progress beyond having a gas turbine that is hydrogen capable. This could include partnerships to secure hydrogen, announcements towards hydrogen infrastructure, or an actual hydrogen contract.
Using hydrogen - The plant is using hydrogen as a fuel.
no - The plant is not capable of using hydrogen. 
not found - no information found
Value of 'yes' if the unit connected to a data center through a PPA. Currently geothermal only data field.
Primary country/area where the project is located (unabbreviated)
fields.notes_from_readme: If project is binational, Country/area 1 is either the country/area listed first in alphabetical order for projects where capacity is shared equally, or the country/area with a larger portion of the total project capacity.

fields.definition_from_readme: Additional country/area where the project is located (unabbreviated)
fields.notes_from_readme: If project is binational, Country/area 2 is either the country/area listed second in alphabetical order for projects where capacity is shared equally, or the country/area with a smaller portion of the total project capacity.

fields.definition_from_readme: Gross capacity, or best available data, of the portion of the project which is located in Country/area 1, in megawatts, rounded to whole number
fields.notes_from_readme: Units: MW. Capacity cutoffs mentioned above apply to the project as a whole, not necessarily to the portions of an international project which are within one country/area boundary.

fields.definition_from_readme: Gross capacity, or best available data, of the portion of the project which is located in Country/area 2, in megawatts, rounded to whole number
fields.notes_from_readme: Units: MW. Capacity cutoffs mentioned above apply to the project as a whole, not necessarily to the portions of an international project which are within one country/area boundary.

fields.definition_from_readme: The name of the river or watercourse on which the project is located

fields.definition_from_readme: Value of 'yes' if a project phase has combined heat and power/cogeneration, 'no' if not, 'not found' or blank if unknown or no data found.
Value of 'yes' if a project has/plans carbon capture and storage (CCS), 'no' if not, 'not found' or blank if unknown or no data found.

top_values: use of ST in CC set
fields.definition_from_readme:
oil/gas options include: 
conversion - converts the steam turbine of an existing plant to burn gas.
replacement - existing plant is retired and replaced with a new  gas-fired plant. 
change of plans - cases in which one or more  units were planned and those plans were cancelled and subsequently gas units were planned at the same location.
use of ST in CC set - cases where there is a steam turbine (usually coal) and is then repurposed to be part of a gas-fired combined cycle unit. Can also be referred to as “repowering” into a combined cycle where gas turbine(s) and HSRGs are added to the existing steam turbine. Was labeled as "partial conversion" in previous GOGPT data releases
not found
bioenergy options include:
gas to bioenergy conversion - when a unit has been or will be converted from gas to bioenergy
coal to bioenergy conversion - when a unit has been or will be converted from coal to bioenergy
oil to bioenergy conversion - when a unit has been or will be converted from fossil oil to bioenergy
no conversion - unit is not associated with a conversion
Year the unit was or is projected to be converted to bioenergy
fields.notes_from_readme: Units: yyyy.

top_values: chemicals, iron & steel, machinery, other metals & mining
fields.definition_from_readme: The type of captive industry use. Options include: agriculture, aluminum, automobiles, cement & building, chemicals, coal mining & coal products, cryptocurrency, dairy, data center, electronics, hydrogen production, industrial park, iron & steel, machinery, nickel, nutrition, oil & refining, other, other metals & mining, pulp & paper, rubber, sugar, textiles, unknown.

"Captive" power plants are those that are within industrial facilities. These power plants typically supply most of their power (and if they're CHP, most of their heat as well) to the industrial facility. Options include: heat, power, both, blank.
Indicate if heat or power is used outside of the captive industrial facility or complex. Options include: heat, power, both, blank, None. 'None' indicates an entirely captive plant.
Latitude of the project, assumed WGS84 (Google Maps)
fields.notes_from_readme: Units: decimal degrees.

fields.notes_from_readme: Units: decimal degrees.
